title: Getting started with gwasplot
text: Every function expects four canonical columns SNP, CHR, POS P (plus optional gene trait). rarely prepare hand — validate_gwas() auto-detects common column-name aliases coerces types: plotting functions call validate_gwas() internally, can pass raw data directly. Two simulated datasets ship package:
code: raw <- data.frame(   marker = c("rs1", "rs2", "rs3"),   chrom  = c(1, 1, 2),   bp     = c(100, 200, 150),   pvalue = c(1e-8, 0.2, 3e-3) ) validate_gwas(raw) #> # A tibble: 3 × 4 #>   SNP   CHR     POS          P #>   <chr> <fct> <dbl>      <dbl> #> 1 rs1   1       100 0.00000001 #> 2 rs2   1       200 0.2        #> 3 rs3   2       150 0.003 data(gwas_example)  # single trait, 22 chromosomes data(gwas_multi)    # three traits: height, bmi, glucose gwas_example #> # A tibble: 5,940 × 5 #>    SNP   CHR       POS      P gene      #>    <chr> <fct>   <dbl>  <dbl> <chr>     #>  1 rs1   1       74399 0.295  GENE01399 #>  2 rs2   1      180831 0.0955 GENE01831 #>  3 rs3   1     1147122 0.165  GENE01122 #>  4 rs4   1     1321963 0.870  GENE01963 #>  5 rs5   1     1547422 0.753  GENE01422 #>  6 rs6   1     1574771 0.815  GENE01771 #>  7 rs7   1     2234627 0.462  GENE01627 #>  8 rs8   1     2374338 0.740  GENE01338 #>  9 rs9   1     2533357 0.726  GENE01357 #> 10 rs10  1     2628801 0.765  GENE01801 #> # ℹ 5,930 more rows

title: Getting started with gwasplot
text: Chromosomes laid end--end alternating colour bands; solid line genome-wide threshold (5e-8) dashed line suggestive threshold (1e-5).
code: gwas_manhattan(gwas_example)

title: Getting started with gwasplot
text: highlight_top() bundles selection rule shared every plot table, figure table always agree. Combine top_n, threshold, snps, genes, :  groups taking top_n — example, lead SNP every chromosome:
code: gwas_manhattan(gwas_example, highlight = highlight_top(top_n = 6)) gwas_manhattan(gwas_example, highlight = highlight_top(top_n = 1, by = "CHR"),                label = FALSE)

title: Getting started with gwasplot
code: gwas_chromosome(gwas_example, chr = 1, highlight = highlight_top(top_n = 3))

title: Getting started with gwasplot
text: gwas_circular() arranges chromosomes around circle draws trait concentric ring (outermost = first trait). CMplot-style view.  Useful options: shared_scale = FALSE scales ring maximum (peaks fill ring) instead common scale. ideogram = FALSE drops central chromosome band. colors sets one colour per ring.  single-trait frame collapses one ring automatically:  Tip. highlight many markers (e.g. every genome-wide hit via threshold), set label = FALSE avoid wall overlapping labels.
code: gwas_circular(gwas_multi, highlight = highlight_top(top_n = 1, by = "trait")) gwas_circular(gwas_multi, shared_scale = FALSE, ideogram = FALSE,               colors = c("#0b6e4f", "#e28413", "#8367c7")) gwas_circular(gwas_example, highlight = highlight_top(top_n = 1, by = "CHR"))

title: Getting started with gwasplot
text: Pass interactive = TRUE plot get ggiraph widget. Hover point see SNP, gene, chromosome, position p-value; scroll zoom.
code: gwas_manhattan(gwas_example, highlight = highlight_top(top_n = 6),                interactive = TRUE)

title: Getting started with gwasplot
text: gwas_top() returns selected markers plain tibble, gwas_table() formats gt table — positions thousands separators, p-values scientific notation, colour-scaled −log⁡10(P)-\log_{10}(P) column. table plot share highlight_top(), can highlight markers :
code: gwas_top(gwas_example, highlight = highlight_top(top_n = 5)) #> # A tibble: 5 × 6 #>   SNP    gene      CHR        POS        P neg_log10_p #>   <chr>  <chr>     <fct>    <dbl>    <dbl>       <dbl> #> 1 rs5486 GENE20370 20     1342370 2.55e-13        12.6 #> 2 rs5485 GENE20950 20      651950 5.50e-13        12.3 #> 3 rs5487 GENE20702 20     1501702 5.50e-13        12.3 #> 4 rs646  GENE02691 2     65881691 7.94e-13        12.1 #> 5 rs5484 GENE20446 20      535446 1.18e-12        11.9 gwas_table(gwas_example, highlight = highlight_top(top_n = 8),            title = "Top GWAS hits") hl <- highlight_top(top_n = 8) gwas_manhattan(gwas_example, highlight = hl)   # figure gwas_table(gwas_example, highlight = hl)        # matching table

title: Authors and Citation
text: loukesio. Author, maintainer.

code: @Manual{,   title = {gwasplot: Publication-Ready and Interactive Plots for GWAS Results},   author = {{loukesio}},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.0.0.9000},   url = {https://github.com/loukesio/gwasplot}, }

what: Publication-Ready and Interactive Plots for GWAS Results
title: Publication-Ready and Interactive Plots for GWAS Results
text: 📖 Documentation & vignette: https://loukesio.github.io/gwasplot/ gwasplot ggplot2-based toolkit visualising genome-wide association study (GWAS) results. aims make easy produce publication-ready interactive figures: Linear Manhattan plots across genome, alternating chromosome colours genome-wide / suggestive significance thresholds. Per-chromosome plots zoom region interest. Circular (CMplot-style) plots support multiple concentric trait rings. Easy top-marker highlighting — threshold, top-N, named SNPs/genes, automatic labelling. Interactive versions via ggiraph (hover tooltips showing SNP, gene, chromosome, position p-value). Companion tables top hits via gt, spirit ggvolc. Status: early development, core pieces work: data contract (validate_gwas()), Manhattan, single-chromosome multi-ring circular plots, interactive versions via ggiraph, gt top-hits table. vignette polish next.

title: Publication-Ready and Interactive Plots for GWAS Results
code: library(gwasplot) data(gwas_example)   # single trait data(gwas_multi)     # three traits, for circular rings  # Manhattan plot, highlighting the top 6 markers gwas_manhattan(gwas_example, highlight = highlight_top(top_n = 6))  # Zoom into a single chromosome gwas_chromosome(gwas_example, chr = 1, highlight = highlight_top(top_n = 3))  # Circular plot with one concentric ring per trait gwas_circular(gwas_multi, highlight = highlight_top(top_n = 1, by = "trait"))

title: Publication-Ready and Interactive Plots for GWAS Results
text: gwas_circular() arranges chromosomes around circle draws trait concentric ring (outermost = first trait). variations — per-ring scaling, custom colours, two-trait comparison, single-trait collapse: Useful options: shared_scale = FALSE (scale ring maximum), ideogram = FALSE (drop central chromosome band), colors (one colour per ring), label = FALSE (recommended highlighting many markers).

title: Publication-Ready and Interactive Plots for GWAS Results
text: plot becomes interactive ggiraph widget interactive = TRUE — hover point see SNP, gene, chromosome, position p-value:
code: gwas_manhattan(gwas_example, highlight = highlight_top(top_n = 6),                interactive = TRUE) gwas_circular(gwas_multi, interactive = TRUE)

title: Publication-Ready and Interactive Plots for GWAS Results
text: gwas_table() builds publication-ready gt table top hits (gwas_top() returns selection plain tibble). shares highlight_top() selection logic plots, table figure always agree:
code: gwas_table(gwas_example, highlight = highlight_top(top_n = 8),            title = "Top GWAS hits")

title: Publication-Ready and Interactive Plots for GWAS Results
code: # install.packages("devtools") devtools::install_github("loukesio/gwasplot")

title: Publication-Ready and Interactive Plots for GWAS Results
text: Every plotting function expects tidy GWAS data frame. validate_gwas() standardises one — auto-detects common column names (e.g. BP → POS, pvalue → P) returns clean tibble SNP, CHR, POS, P (plus optional gene trait). can override detection explicitly, e.g. validate_gwas(df, p = "P_BOLT_LMM").
code: library(gwasplot)  df <- data.frame(   marker = c("rs1", "rs2", "rs3"),   chrom  = c(1, 1, 2),   bp     = c(100, 200, 150),   pvalue = c(1e-8, 0.2, 3e-3) )  validate_gwas(df)

title: Publication-Ready and Interactive Plots for GWAS Results
text: Two simulated datasets ship package:
code: data(gwas_example)  # single trait, 22 chromosomes data(gwas_multi)    # three traits, for multi-ring circular plots

title: Publication-Ready and Interactive Plots for GWAS Results
text: MIT © gwasplot authors

what: Single-chromosome (regional) plot of GWAS results — gwas_chromosome
title: Single-chromosome (regional) plot of GWAS results — gwas_chromosome
text: Zooms one chromosome (optionally base-pair window), plotting \(-\log_{10}(P)\) position megabases. Significance lines top-marker highlighting work gwas_manhattan().

title: Single-chromosome (regional) plot of GWAS results — gwas_chromosome
code: gwas_chromosome(   data,   chr,   xlim = NULL,   threshold = 5e-08,   suggestive = 1e-05,   highlight = NULL,   label = TRUE,   label_by = c("gene", "SNP"),   point_color = "#2f5c8f",   highlight_color = "#d1422f",   point_size = 1.4,   point_alpha = 0.85,   interactive = FALSE,   title = NULL,   subtitle = NULL )

title: Single-chromosome (regional) plot of GWAS results — gwas_chromosome
text: data GWAS data frame. passed validate_gwas(), raw column names (e.g. BP, pvalue) accepted. chr chromosome plot (matched CHR column, without "chr" prefix). xlim Optional numeric length-2 base-pair window c(start, end) restrict region shown. threshold Genome-wide significance p-value drawn solid line (default 5e-8). Use NULL omit. suggestive Suggestive significance p-value drawn dashed line (default 1e-5). Use NULL omit. highlight highlight_top() specification, single number (shorthand top_n), NULL highlighting. Highlighted markers drawn highlight_color. label Logical; label highlighted markers (default TRUE highlight given). Requires highlighted set non-empty. label_by Column used labels, "gene" (default, falls back "SNP" absent) "SNP". point_color Colour (non-highlighted) points. highlight_color Colour highlighted markers labels. point_size, point_alpha Size alpha non-highlighted points. interactive Logical; TRUE, return interactive girafe widget hover tooltips (requires ggiraph). title, subtitle Optional plot title subtitle.

title: Single-chromosome (regional) plot of GWAS results — gwas_chromosome
text: ggplot2::ggplot object, ggiraph::girafe() htmlwidget interactive = TRUE.

title: Single-chromosome (regional) plot of GWAS results — gwas_chromosome
code: data(gwas_example) gwas_chromosome(gwas_example, chr = 1)  gwas_chromosome(gwas_example, chr = 1, highlight = highlight_top(top_n = 3))

what: Circular (CMplot-style) plot of GWAS results, with multiple trait rings — gwas_circular
title: Circular (CMplot-style) plot of GWAS results, with multiple trait rings — gwas_circular
text: Draws circular Manhattan plot: chromosomes arranged around circle trait shown concentric ring, outside . Genome-wide significance rings top-marker highlighting supported per trait. multi-ring analogue gwas_manhattan().

title: Circular (CMplot-style) plot of GWAS results, with multiple trait rings — gwas_circular
code: gwas_circular(   data,   trait = "trait",   threshold = 5e-08,   highlight = NULL,   label = TRUE,   label_by = c("gene", "SNP"),   colors = NULL,   highlight_color = "#d1422f",   ideogram = TRUE,   point_size = 0.9,   point_alpha = 0.85,   shared_scale = TRUE,   r_inner = 0.38,   ring_gap = 0.035,   interactive = FALSE,   title = NULL,   subtitle = NULL )

title: Circular (CMplot-style) plot of GWAS results, with multiple trait rings — gwas_circular
text: data GWAS data frame (passed validate_gwas()). multiple rings must contain trait column, can bind several single-trait frames together set trait. trait Column splits data rings (default "trait" present, otherwise single ring). Set NULL force single ring. threshold Genome-wide significance p-value drawn ring per trait (default 5e-8; NULL omit). highlight highlight_top() spec, single number (top_n), NULL. Applied per trait. label Logical; label highlighted markers (default TRUE). label_by Column labels, "gene" (default) "SNP". colors Optional vector ring colours (one per trait). Recycled / truncated needed; defaults built-palette. highlight_color Colour highlighted markers labels. ideogram Logical; draw central chromosome colour band (default TRUE). point_size, point_alpha Size alpha points. shared_scale Logical; TRUE (default) rings share one \(-\log_{10}(P)\) scale ring heights comparable. FALSE ring scaled maximum. r_inner Innermost radius ring stack (0-1); space inside used ideogram labels. ring_gap Radial gap adjacent rings (0-1). interactive Logical; TRUE, return interactive girafe widget per-point hover tooltips (requires ggiraph). title, subtitle Optional title subtitle.

title: Circular (CMplot-style) plot of GWAS results, with multiple trait rings — gwas_circular
text: ggplot2::ggplot object, ggiraph::girafe() htmlwidget interactive = TRUE.

title: Circular (CMplot-style) plot of GWAS results, with multiple trait rings — gwas_circular
code: data(gwas_multi) gwas_circular(gwas_multi)                                   # 3 rings  gwas_circular(gwas_multi, highlight = highlight_top(top_n = 1, by = "trait"))   data(gwas_example) gwas_circular(gwas_example)                                 # single ring

title: Example single-trait GWAS results — gwas_example
text: simulated genome-wide association study 22 chromosomes uniform null background several injected association peaks. Generated simulate_gwas() using fixed seed; see data-raw/make_example_data.R.

title: Example single-trait GWAS results — gwas_example
code: gwas_example

title: Example single-trait GWAS results — gwas_example
text: tibble one row per SNP columns: SNP Marker identifier (character). CHR Chromosome, ordered factor natural genomic order. POS Base-pair position within chromosome (numeric). P Association p-value (numeric, (0, 1]). gene Nearest-gene label (character).

title: Example single-trait GWAS results — gwas_example
text: Simulated simulate_gwas().

what: Manhattan plot of GWAS results — gwas_manhattan
title: Manhattan plot of GWAS results — gwas_manhattan
text: Draws classic linear Manhattan plot: \(-\log_{10}(P)\) genome position, chromosomes laid end--end coloured alternating bands. Genome-wide suggestive significance lines drawn default, top markers can highlighted labelled.

title: Manhattan plot of GWAS results — gwas_manhattan
code: gwas_manhattan(   data,   threshold = 5e-08,   suggestive = 1e-05,   highlight = NULL,   label = TRUE,   label_by = c("gene", "SNP"),   colors = c("#2f5c8f", "#a7bcd6"),   highlight_color = "#d1422f",   point_size = 1,   point_alpha = 0.8,   ylim = NULL,   interactive = FALSE,   title = NULL,   subtitle = NULL )

title: Manhattan plot of GWAS results — gwas_manhattan
text: data GWAS data frame. passed validate_gwas(), raw column names (e.g. BP, pvalue) accepted. threshold Genome-wide significance p-value drawn solid line (default 5e-8). Use NULL omit. suggestive Suggestive significance p-value drawn dashed line (default 1e-5). Use NULL omit. highlight highlight_top() specification, single number (shorthand top_n), NULL highlighting. Highlighted markers drawn highlight_color. label Logical; label highlighted markers (default TRUE highlight given). Requires highlighted set non-empty. label_by Column used labels, "gene" (default, falls back "SNP" absent) "SNP". colors Length-2 vector alternating chromosome-band colours. highlight_color Colour highlighted markers labels. point_size, point_alpha Size alpha non-highlighted points. ylim Optional numeric length-2 y-axis limit \(-\log_{10}(P)\) scale. interactive Logical; TRUE, points carry hover tooltips (SNP, gene, chromosome, position, p-value) plot returned interactive girafe widget. Requires ggiraph package. title, subtitle Optional plot title subtitle.

title: Manhattan plot of GWAS results — gwas_manhattan
text: ggplot2::ggplot object, ggiraph::girafe() htmlwidget interactive = TRUE.

title: Manhattan plot of GWAS results — gwas_manhattan
code: data(gwas_example) gwas_manhattan(gwas_example)  gwas_manhattan(gwas_example, highlight = highlight_top(top_n = 5))

title: Example multi-trait GWAS results — gwas_multi
text: simulated study three traits sharing genome layout, injected association peaks. Intended multi-ring circular plot (gwas_circular()). Generated simulate_gwas(); see data-raw/make_example_data.R.

title: Example multi-trait GWAS results — gwas_multi
code: gwas_multi

title: Example multi-trait GWAS results — gwas_multi
text: tibble one row per SNP--trait columns: SNP Marker identifier (character). CHR Chromosome, ordered factor natural genomic order. POS Base-pair position within chromosome (numeric). P Association p-value (numeric, (0, 1]). gene Nearest-gene label (character). trait Trait / phenotype name (character): one "height", "bmi", "glucose".

title: Example multi-trait GWAS results — gwas_multi
text: Simulated simulate_gwas().

what: A formatted table of the top GWAS markers — gwas_table
title: A formatted table of the top GWAS markers — gwas_table
text: Builds publication-ready gt table top hits, mirroring top-markers table ggvolc package. Positions shown thousands separators, p-values scientific notation, \(-\log_{10}(P)\) optionally colour-scaled.

title: A formatted table of the top GWAS markers — gwas_table
code: gwas_table(   data,   highlight = highlight_top(top_n = 10),   columns = NULL,   color = TRUE,   title = NULL,   subtitle = NULL )

title: A formatted table of the top GWAS markers — gwas_table
text: data GWAS data frame (passed validate_gwas()). highlight highlight_top() spec, single number (top_n). Defaults 10 smallest-p markers. columns Optional character vector columns keep (order). default keeps SNP, gene, trait, CHR, POS, P, neg_log10_p present. color Logical; colour neg_log10_p column value (default TRUE). title, subtitle Optional table title subtitle.

title: A formatted table of the top GWAS markers — gwas_table
text: gt table object (class gt_tbl, see gt::gt()). Requires gt package.

title: A formatted table of the top GWAS markers — gwas_table

what: Extract the top GWAS markers as a tidy table — gwas_top
title: Extract the top GWAS markers as a tidy table — gwas_top
text: Returns top hits selected highlight_top() specification, ordered p-value, neg_log10_p column added. data behind gwas_table() uses selection logic plots' highlight argument, table figure can kept perfectly sync.

title: Extract the top GWAS markers as a tidy table — gwas_top
code: gwas_top(data, highlight = highlight_top(top_n = 10), columns = NULL)

title: Extract the top GWAS markers as a tidy table — gwas_top
text: data GWAS data frame (passed validate_gwas()). highlight highlight_top() spec, single number (top_n). Defaults 10 smallest-p markers. columns Optional character vector columns keep (order). default keeps SNP, gene, trait, CHR, POS, P, neg_log10_p present.

title: Extract the top GWAS markers as a tidy table — gwas_top
text: tibble selected markers.

title: Extract the top GWAS markers as a tidy table — gwas_top
code: data(gwas_example) gwas_top(gwas_example, highlight = highlight_top(top_n = 5)) #> # A tibble: 5 × 6 #>   SNP    gene      CHR        POS        P neg_log10_p #>   <chr>  <chr>     <fct>    <dbl>    <dbl>       <dbl> #> 1 rs5486 GENE20370 20     1342370 2.55e-13        12.6 #> 2 rs5485 GENE20950 20      651950 5.50e-13        12.3 #> 3 rs5487 GENE20702 20     1501702 5.50e-13        12.3 #> 4 rs646  GENE02691 2     65881691 7.94e-13        12.1 #> 5 rs5484 GENE20446 20      535446 1.18e-12        11.9

what: gwasplot: Publication-Ready and Interactive Plots for GWAS Results — gwasplot-package
title: gwasplot: Publication-Ready and Interactive Plots for GWAS Results — gwasplot-package
text: ggplot2-based toolkit visualising genome-wide association study (GWAS) results. Provides linear Manhattan plots, per-chromosome plots circular (CMplot-style) plots support multiple concentric trait rings, easy highlighting labelling top markers, interactive versions via 'ggiraph', companion tables top hits via 'gt'.

title: gwasplot: Publication-Ready and Interactive Plots for GWAS Results — gwasplot-package
text: Maintainer: loukesio loukesio@gmail.com

what: Specify which top markers to highlight — highlight_top
title: Specify which top markers to highlight — highlight_top
text: small constructor bundles highlight specification reuse across plotting functions top-hits table. Criteria combined union: marker highlighted satisfies supplied rules.

title: Specify which top markers to highlight — highlight_top
code: highlight_top(   top_n = NULL,   threshold = NULL,   snps = NULL,   genes = NULL,   by = NULL )

title: Specify which top markers to highlight — highlight_top
text: top_n Integer; highlight top_n markers smallest p-values (per group set). threshold Numeric p-value; highlight markers P <= threshold. snps, genes Character vectors SNP ids / gene names highlight. Optional grouping column applied top_n (e.g. "CHR" top hit chromosome, "trait").

title: Specify which top markers to highlight — highlight_top
text: object class "gwas_highlight" (list criteria).

title: Specify which top markers to highlight — highlight_top
code: highlight_top(top_n = 10) #> $top_n #> [1] 10 #>  #> $threshold #> NULL #>  #> $snps #> NULL #>  #> $genes #> NULL #>  #> $by #> NULL #>  #> attr(,"class") #> [1] "gwas_highlight" highlight_top(threshold = 5e-8, genes = c("GENE01000")) #> $top_n #> NULL #>  #> $threshold #> [1] 5e-08 #>  #> $snps #> NULL #>  #> $genes #> [1] "GENE01000" #>  #> $by #> NULL #>  #> attr(,"class") #> [1] "gwas_highlight" highlight_top(top_n = 1, by = "CHR")  # lead SNP per chromosome #> $top_n #> [1] 1 #>  #> $threshold #> NULL #>  #> $snps #> NULL #>  #> $genes #> NULL #>  #> $by #> [1] "CHR" #>  #> attr(,"class") #> [1] "gwas_highlight"

what: Order chromosome labels in natural genomic order — order_chromosomes
title: Order chromosome labels in natural genomic order — order_chromosomes
text: Numeric chromosomes sorted ascending, followed sex mitochondrial chromosomes (X, Y, XY, MT/M) finally labels alphabetically. leading "chr" prefix ignored sorting preserved returned levels.

title: Order chromosome labels in natural genomic order — order_chromosomes
code: order_chromosomes(x)

title: Order chromosome labels in natural genomic order — order_chromosomes
text: x vector chromosome labels.

title: Order chromosome labels in natural genomic order — order_chromosomes
text: character vector unique labels natural order, suitable use factor levels.

title: Order chromosome labels in natural genomic order — order_chromosomes
code: order_chromosomes(c("chr2", "chr10", "chrX", "chr1")) #> [1] "chr1"  "chr2"  "chr10" "chrX"

what: Simulate a GWAS summary-statistics data frame — simulate_gwas
title: Simulate a GWAS summary-statistics data frame — simulate_gwas
text: Generates reproducible, tidy GWAS-style data frame uniform background null p-values plus handful injected association peaks. used build package's bundled example datasets handy examples, tests quickly trying plotting functions.

title: Simulate a GWAS summary-statistics data frame — simulate_gwas
code: simulate_gwas(   n_chr = 22,   snps_per_chr = 1000,   n_peaks = 8,   traits = NULL,   seed = NULL )

title: Simulate a GWAS summary-statistics data frame — simulate_gwas
text: n_chr Number chromosomes. snps_per_chr Approximate number SNPs per chromosome. Chromosome lengths (hence SNP counts) taper higher-numbered chromosomes. n_peaks Number association peaks inject across genome. traits Optional character vector trait names. supplied (length > 1), returned frame gains trait column independent peaks per trait — suitable multi-ring circular plots. seed Optional integer seed reproducibility.

title: Simulate a GWAS summary-statistics data frame — simulate_gwas
text: tibble columns SNP, CHR, POS, P, gene, (traits length > 1) trait.

title: Simulate a GWAS summary-statistics data frame — simulate_gwas

what: A clean theme for gwasplot figures — theme_gwasplot
title: A clean theme for gwasplot figures — theme_gwasplot
text: minimal ggplot2::theme_minimal()-based theme vertical grid lines, tidy axis titles subtle significance-line look. Used default gwas_manhattan(); add override like ggplot2 theme.

title: A clean theme for gwasplot figures — theme_gwasplot
code: theme_gwasplot(base_size = 12, base_family = "")

title: A clean theme for gwasplot figures — theme_gwasplot
text: base_size Base font size, passed ggplot2::theme_minimal(). base_family Base font family.

title: A clean theme for gwasplot figures — theme_gwasplot
text: ggplot2 theme object.

title: A clean theme for gwasplot figures — theme_gwasplot
code: library(ggplot2) #> Warning: package ‘ggplot2’ was built under R version 4.5.2 ggplot(mtcars, aes(wt, mpg)) + geom_point() + theme_gwasplot()

what: Validate and standardise a GWAS data frame — validate_gwas
title: Validate and standardise a GWAS data frame — validate_gwas
text: Checks data frame contains columns required gwasplot returns tidy, type-coerced tibble canonical column names. Column names auto-detected set common aliases (example BP position map POS; pvalue maps P) can supplied explicitly.

title: Validate and standardise a GWAS data frame — validate_gwas
code: validate_gwas(   data,   snp = NULL,   chr = NULL,   pos = NULL,   p = NULL,   gene = NULL,   trait = NULL,   na.rm = TRUE )

title: Validate and standardise a GWAS data frame — validate_gwas
text: data data frame GWAS summary statistics. snp, chr, pos, p Optional strings giving column names use role, overriding auto-detection. gene, trait Optional strings giving column names use (optional) gene trait roles. na.rm Logical; drop rows missing CHR, POS P (default TRUE).

title: Validate and standardise a GWAS data frame — validate_gwas
text: tibble columns SNP, CHR (ordered factor), POS (numeric), P (numeric) first, followed gene /trait present, remaining columns data.

title: Validate and standardise a GWAS data frame — validate_gwas
text: canonical contract four required columns — SNP (marker id), CHR (chromosome), POS (base-pair position) P (p-value) — plus two optional columns, gene trait. CHR returned ordered factor natural genomic order (see order_chromosomes()). additional columns data preserved, convenient interactive tooltips.

title: Validate and standardise a GWAS data frame — validate_gwas
code: df <- data.frame(   marker = c("rs1", "rs2", "rs3"),   chrom  = c(1, 1, 2),   bp     = c(100, 200, 150),   pvalue = c(1e-8, 0.2, 3e-3) ) validate_gwas(df) #> # A tibble: 3 × 4 #>   SNP   CHR     POS          P #>   <chr> <fct> <dbl>      <dbl> #> 1 rs1   1       100 0.00000001 #> 2 rs2   1       200 0.2        #> 3 rs3   2       150 0.003
